title: Background Remover: Paint-to-Select + Erase Button Workflow

requirements.summary: Add paint-to-select + erase button workflow to Background Remover module, allowing users to paint over areas then click an Erase button to remove only the painted pixels
acceptanceCriteria: A Paint button is visible in the Background Remover module that activates brush/paint mode on the canvas.
When paint mode is active, the user can paint over any area of the image and the painted region is highlighted with a semi-transparent red overlay.
After painting, an 'Erase' button becomes visible/enabled in the panel.

Clicking the Erase button removes (erases) only the pixels covered by the painted brush strokes — making them transparent for PNG output or replacing them with the selected background color for JPG output.
The result is immediately reflected in the canvas preview and the before/after preview.
The Erase button is disabled or hidden when no brush strokes have been painted (empty mask).
The existing Auto Detect and Remove Background buttons in RemovalPanel continue to function independently.
The Erase button follows the existing tool-btn glassmorphism styling with teal/cyan accent colors.

file_operations.description: Add a new `processManualErase` function that processes the current mask canvas and removes only the painted pixels from the source image. This function must: (1) Read the mask canvas pixel data to identify painted regions (red channel > 0). (2) Apply the mask to the source image by making matching pixels transparent (for PNG) or replacing them with the selected background color (for JPG). (3) Update the resultCanvas with the processed image. (4) Clear the mask canvas after processing. (5) Create a snapshot for undo history. Return the function from the hook along with a computed `hasMaskStrokes` boolean that checks if the mask canvas contains any painted pixels.

Add a new 'Erase' button to the BrushToolPanel UI that: (1) Is positioned prominently after the existing brush controls. (2) Is conditionally rendered/enabled only when `hasMaskStrokes` is true (mask is non-empty). (3) Calls the `processManualErase` function from the useBackgroundRemover hook when clicked. (4) Uses the existing tool-btn styling with teal/cyan accent colors (bg-cyan-500/20 hover:bg-cyan-500/30 border-cyan-500/40). (5) Displays appropriate icon and label text such as 'Erase Selected Area' or 'Apply Erase'. Ensure the button is visually distinct from the existing erase-mode toggle button.

Update the BackgroundRemover component to: (1) Destructure the new `processManualErase` and `hasMaskStrokes` values from the useBackgroundRemover hook. (2) Pass `processManualErase` and `hasMaskStrokes` as props to the BrushToolPanel component. Ensure the existing brush tool workflow (paint mode, erase mode toggle, undo) remains unchanged and functional.
